user_query:
I’m a business major exploring a startup concept inspired by historical trade routes. Please help me turn my rough notes into an evidence-based critique grounded in structured data extracted from the provided local files. Specifically:

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/trade_routes.html (an HTML page with a table id="routes" listing selected historical trade routes)
- input/competitor_profiles.csv (CSV of current competitor profiles)
- input/startup_concept.md (my draft concept document)

Requirements:
1) Create and run a script at scripts/extract_and_compare.py that accepts CLI arguments in this order:
   python scripts/extract_and_compare.py input/trade_routes.html input/competitor_profiles.csv outputs/routes_extracted.csv outputs/competitor_overlap.csv

2) The script must:
   - Parse the HTML table with id="routes" in input/trade_routes.html and produce outputs/routes_extracted.csv with one row per route and the following columns:
     route_id, era, origin, destination, transport_mode, commodities, commodities_count, seasonality, risk_score, tolls, risk_adjusted_toll, viable
     Where:
     - commodities: pipe-delimited list of commodities from the HTML, sorted alphabetically (e.g., books|gold|salt)
     - commodities_count: count of unique commodities on the route
     - risk_adjusted_toll = tolls * (1 + risk_score/10)
     - viable: yes if risk_adjusted_toll <= 140, otherwise no
   - Read input/competitor_profiles.csv and produce outputs/competitor_overlap.csv with columns:
     competitor, overlapping_commodities, overlap_count, coverage_of_viable_percent
     Where:
     - overlapping_commodities is the pipe-delimited alphabetical intersection between the competitor’s commodities and the union of commodities that appear on viable routes in outputs/routes_extracted.csv
     - overlap_count is the number of items in overlapping_commodities
     - coverage_of_viable_percent is the percentage (rounded to the nearest whole percent) of viable routes that include at least one commodity from overlapping_commodities

3) Rewrite the concept document by creating outputs/startup_concept_review.md. Start from the original input/startup_concept.md content (preserve all existing sections and text), then append these sections:
   - A level-2 heading "Evidence-based Critique" that:
     • States the number of viable routes (count of rows with viable = yes) and the average risk_adjusted_toll across all routes from outputs/routes_extracted.csv.
     • Lists the top 3 commodities by occurrence across viable routes (break ties alphabetically), and briefly explains what that implies for our go-to-market.
     • Identifies the top 2 competitors by coverage_of_viable_percent from outputs/competitor_overlap.csv (break ties by higher overlap_count, then alphabetically) and summarizes where they may pressure our positioning.
   - A level-2 heading "Top 3 Learnings" followed by a two-column markdown table with columns "Finding" and "Implication for the concept", with three rows grounded in the extracted data and overlaps.

Constraints:
- Use only the provided local files; no Internet lookups.
- The number of rows in outputs/routes_extracted.csv must match the number of data rows in the HTML table body (id="routes").
- All route_id values in outputs/routes_extracted.csv must exactly match those in input/trade_routes.html.
- Ensure all output files are created at the specified paths under outputs/.

Deliverables:
- scripts/extract_and_compare.py
- outputs/routes_extracted.csv
- outputs/competitor_overlap.csv
- outputs/startup_concept_review.md
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
